steps.title: Identify what the assertion needs
steps.content:
The passage asserts that flies collected in cooler months tend to have a shorter lifespan, because smaller wing centroid size (the proxy for body size) correlates with shorter lifespan. To illustrate this, the data must show smaller wing sizes in a cooler month compared to a warmer one.
  • "the average female wing centroid size was smaller in May than in June.": May is cooler than June, and a smaller wing size in the cooler month directly supports the assertion that cooler months produce smaller (shorter-lived) flies. ✓
  • "the average male wing centroid size was consistently smaller than the average female wing centroid size in all four months in the table.": This compares males to females across months, not cooler months to warmer ones — irrelevant to the temperature-lifespan claim. ✗
  • "the average monthly low temperature was lower in May than in June.": This only restates that May is cooler; it says nothing about wing size or lifespan. ✗
  • "the average male wing centroid size was 1.98 mm in May but was 2.31 mm in July.": This compares male wing sizes across months but does not address the cooler-month, shorter-lifespan relationship for female flies. ✗

steps.title: Confirm the match
steps.content: Only the average female wing centroid size was smaller in May than in June. links a cooler month (May) to a smaller wing centroid size, which directly illustrates the assertion that cooler months produce shorter-lived flies. That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
